commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content:
as of a lamb without blemish and without spot. Here he touches on the ancient Levitical and sacerdotal celebrations; but means a soul pure through righteousness which is offered to God.

Verily foreknown before the foundation of the world. Inasmuch as He was foreknown before every creature, because He was Christ. But manifested in the last times by the generation of a body. Being born again, not of corruptible seed. The soul, then, which is produced along with the body is corruptible, as some think.

But the word of the Lord, he says, endures for ever: as well prophecy as divine doctrine.

But you are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood.

commentaries.author: Lucius Caecilius Firmianus Lactantius
commentaries.content: For when God was about to smite the Egyptians, to secure the Hebrews from that infliction He had enjoined them to slay a white

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: And, for fear you should fly to that statement for a licence to fornication, on the ground that you will be sinning against a thing which is yours, not the Lord's, he takes you away from yourself, and awards you, according to his previous disposition, to Christ: "And ye are not your own; "immediately opposing (thereto), "for bought ye are with a price"-the blood, to wit, of the Lord:

commentaries.author: The Apostolic Constitutions
commentaries.content: Let the bishop pray for the people, and say: "Save Thy people, O Lord, and bless Thine inheritance, which Thou hast obtained with the precious blood of Thy Christ, and hast called a royal priesthood, and an holy nation."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
